semanticCore: Gentleness, softness, tender, palm tree, ease, or flexible
quranicRelevance: Soft/gentle speech (Layyin - 'qawlan layyinan'), behaving gently (Linta - 'linta lahum'), and soft palm trees (Leenah - 'leenatin').

keyDerivations.meaning: To grow soft, gentle, yielding, tender, or malleable
To make soft, malleable, flexible, or yielding
Soft, excellent palm tree (yielding soft sweet dates)

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَبِمَا رَحْمَةٍ مِنَ اللَّهِ لِنْتَ لَهُمْ (3:159): So by mercy from God you (O Prophet) behaved gently/softly with them (gentle leadership).
وَأَلَنَّا لَهُ الْحَدِيدَ (34:10): And We made the iron soft/malleable for him (David's technological miracle).
summaryEn: The root denotes gentleness and softness, contrasting the Prophet's gentle leadership (Linta) and soft speech with David's soft, malleable iron (Alanna).
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الليونة والرقة والنعومة، واصفاً لين القيادة النبوية الشريفة (لِنت لهم)، وتليين الحديد لداود (وألنّا)، والنخيل اللينة المقطوعة.
rawText: أَلَانَ بَطْنَهُ 4 [It relaxed his bowels]; said of medicine. (K in art. سهل.) اِسْتَلَانَ 10 : see its contr. استخشن. عَيْشٌ لَيِّنٌ Soft, delicate, or easy, life. === لَيِّنُ الجَانِبِ: see جَانِبٌ. === لَيِّنُ الأَعْطَافِ; &c.: see art. عطف. لَانَ 1 , inf. n. لِينٌ [not لَيْنٌ as in the CK] and لَياَنٌ, (T, S, M, K,) contr. of خَشُنَ; (S, K in art. خشن, and TK;) It was, or became, soft, as opposed to rough or harsh; smooth; plain; without asperities; fine to the touch; delicate; tender; supple; lithe; limber; pliant; pliable; flexible; ductile; malleable; soft, or flabby; lax: and he was, or became, soft; tender; pliant; gentle; bland; or mild. Hence لان جَانِبُهُ: see لَيِّنٌ. === لَانَ بَطْنُهُ His bowels became relaxed. === لَانَ He relented. لَانَ for الْآنَ: see the latter in art. اين. لَايَنَهُ بِالقَوْلِ 3 He soothed, coaxed, or wheedled, him with words. (L, art. مسح.) === لَايَنَهُ [He acted gently towards him; (M, K;) treated him with gentleness, or blandishment; soothed him; coaxed him; wheedled him;] i. q. دَارَاهُ. (S, M, Msb, voce داراه.) === لَايَنَهُ He was soft, tender, gentle, bland, or mild, towards him. (M, K.) لِينَةٌ applied to a palm-tree: see art. لون; and see عَجْوَةٌ. لِينُ العَيْشِ Softness, delicateness, or easiness, of life. مُلَيِّنٌ A lenitive, or laxative, medicine.
